transition.intent: Move from routine growth operations to measured-growth readiness only after content changes are clean, buildable, and supported by supplied analytics evidence.
transition.decision_rule: The site must build, the working tree must be clean, and analytics-dependent gates must not be guessed.

phase_model.purpose: Operate the existing scheduled post queue, improve internal links, and strengthen AI coding agent operation hubs without mass-producing content.
blocked_work: invented Search Console or GA4 data
large theme changes without explicit request
exit_criteria: human-supplied Search Console or GA4 evidence is available

phase_model.purpose: Use supplied query and traffic data to prioritize measured content changes.
blocked_work: large cannibalization cleanup without scoped evidence

phase_model.purpose: Keep the site buildable and policy-aligned when no growth scope is selected.
blocked_work: new content generation without a selected topic
exit_criteria: a new explicit growth task is selected

required_gates.description: Work must remain on master unless the user explicitly requests otherwise.
The repository currently has pre-existing uncommitted content changes. Do not transition phases until those are resolved by the owner or automation scope.
required_gates.on_failure: Leave existing dirty files untouched and keep growth-operations phase.

required_gates.description: The Jekyll build must pass before transition.
Default work should operate the scheduled queue and avoid broad new post volume.
Search Console or GA4 data must be supplied before measured-growth decisions.
required_gates.on_failure: Record missing analytics as user input needed instead of estimating.

required_gates.description: Public pages must not link to unpublished future-dated posts by default.
